entries.summary: Root package manifest for the sdd-forge npm package, defining entry points, scripts, and published file set.
entries.detail: Declares the package name, version, and module type (ES modules). The 'files' field restricts published content to 'src/' plus top-level metadata files. Defines CLI bin entry, npm scripts for testing and building, and lists no external runtime dependencies in accordance with the project's no-external-deps policy.

entries.summary: Top-level dispatcher for the `sdd-forge docs` subcommand group, routing CLI arguments to individual documentation pipeline commands. Orchestrates the full build pipeline including scan, enrich, init, data, text, readme, and agents phases.
entries.detail: Imports and dynamically dispatches to subcommands (scan, enrich, init, data, text, readme, agents, translate) using lazy dynamic imports keyed by a SCRIPTS map. Handles multi-language docs generation by iterating over configured languages and running init/data/readme for each language subdirectory. Supports flags such as --force and --dry-run, propagating them via a shared baseCtx object. Uses resolveCommandContext and resolveOutputConfig to build the execution context, and exits with EXIT_ERROR on unknown or failed commands. Also supports passthrough execution of arbitrary script paths for extensibility.

entries.summary: Generates and refines the AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md file for a project using AI, merging SDD template content with project-specific context.
entries.detail: Reads the existing AGENTS.md (or creates a stub), resolves {{data}} directives in the SDD section, then calls an AI agent with a refine prompt built from package.json scripts, docs content, and README to produce an updated PROJECT section. Uses directive-parser to isolate and replace only the PROJECT block, preserving the SDD section. Writes the result back to AGENTS.md.

entries.summary: Generates a Markdown changelog by parsing spec directories and extracting metadata from spec.md files.
entries.detail: Scans the 'specs/' directory for numbered subdirectories, reads each spec.md, and extracts title, status, date, and description fields. Sanitizes text to remove HTML tags and normalises whitespace. Outputs a formatted Markdown table to docs/change_log.md or a custom path. Supports --lang and --out CLI flags.

entries.summary: Populates {{data}} directives in docs chapter files by resolving them against analysis.json via DataSource resolvers.
entries.detail: Loads analysis.json from the .sdd-forge output directory, creates a resolver via createResolver(), and iterates over each chapter file calling resolveDataDirectives(). Applies file-context rules so that a chapter file only sees analysis entries belonging to its category. Writes changed files back to disk and logs a summary. Exports populateFromAnalysis() for reuse by forge.js.

entries.summary: AI-powered enrichment step that annotates each analysis.json entry with summary, detail, chapter classification, and keywords using a batched LLM call.
entries.detail: Collects all entries from analysis.json, splits them into token-limited batches, and calls the configured AI agent asynchronously with concurrency control via mapWithConcurrency(). Parses the JSON response using repairJson(), merges enriched fields back into the analysis, and saves incremental progress to disk. Supports --dry-run, --dump, and --reset flags. Skips already-enriched entries unless --force is specified.

entries.summary: Orchestrates AI-driven documentation authoring with an iterative write-review loop across multiple chapter files.
entries.detail: Supports three modes: local (per-file agent calls), assist, and agent. Builds a system prompt from analysis summary and optional spec content, then invokes the AI agent concurrently per target file using mapWithConcurrency(). After each round, runs the review command and feeds failure output back as reviewFeedback in subsequent rounds up to --max-runs. Finalises by regenerating README.md and translations.

entries.summary: Initialises the docs/ directory by resolving preset templates, merging chapter lists, and writing chapter Markdown files.
entries.detail: Resolves the template chain from the project's preset type, merges preset and config chapter lists via resolveChaptersOrder(), and optionally calls an AI agent to filter chapters based on analysis content. Translates template content when lang != 'en'. Skips files that already exist unless --force is passed. Writes each chapter file with stripped block directives.

entries.summary: Generates or updates the project's README.md by merging the readme template, resolving data directives, and filling text directives with AI.
entries.detail: Resolves the readme template from the preset chain, applies resolveDataDirectives() with a path-aware resolver, and calls processTemplate() from text.js for {{text}} blocks. Handles both root README.md and per-language README files via ctx.output. Preserves the existing file if the generated content is identical to avoid spurious writes.

entries.summary: Validates generated documentation quality by checking for unexpanded directives, short files, broken HTML comments, and analysis coverage.
entries.detail: Iterates over all chapter files and checks minimum line count, exposed {{data}}/{{text}} directives, broken HTML comments, and residual block tags via checkOutputIntegrity(). Verifies that README.md exists and that all translated language docs exist and are non-empty. Cross-checks analysis.json entry coverage against docs content. Throws on any failure so it can be used as a CI gate.

entries.summary: Scans source files using preset-defined DataSources and writes a structured analysis.json capturing file metadata and categorised entries.
entries.detail: Loads DataSource modules from preset chain directories, runs each source to collect categorised file entries, and assigns stable IDs by matching against an existing analysis.json index. Computes MD5 hashes of source and data-source files to detect staleness. Supports --reset to clear specific categories, --stdout for piped output, and merges new results with the existing JSON to preserve enriched fields.

entries.summary: Implements the `sdd-forge text` command that fills `{{text}}` directives in docs Markdown files by invoking an AI agent with source analysis context. Supports both single-directive and batch JSON processing modes.
entries.detail: Reads each chapter file, parses `{{text}}` directives via `parseDirectives`, and sends prompts to the configured AI agent using `callAgent`/`callAgentAsync`. Batch mode submits all directives in a single JSON-structured request and applies results via `applyBatchJsonToFile`. Includes shrinkage detection to reject AI responses that are suspiciously shorter than the original. Uses `detectChangedChapters` to skip unchanged files for incremental rebuilds based on file hash comparison. Exports `stripFillContent` to remove previously generated content before regeneration.

entries.summary: Translates existing docs chapter files and README.md into configured target languages using an AI agent.
entries.detail: Builds a task list of source→target file pairs, skipping files where the target is newer than the source unless --force is set. Calls callAgentAsync() concurrently via mapWithConcurrency() with a language-specific tone instruction. Strips response preamble and writes translated content to per-language subdirectories under docs/. Creates language subdirectories automatically.

entries.summary: DataSource providing agent-related metadata including the SDD template content, configured language, and package scripts.
entries.detail: Extends DataSource to expose methods used by {{data}} directives in AGENTS.md templates. Reads the SDD template from the base preset's AGENTS.sdd.md for the configured language. Loads config.json from .sdd-forge/ and package.json to provide agent configuration and script listings. Formats the last-generated timestamp via formatUTCTimestamp().

entries.summary: DataSource providing documentation structure metadata: chapter lists, navigation links, language-relative paths, and repo URLs.
entries.detail: Extends DataSource to expose chapter file listings, prev/next navigation links, and cross-language relative path resolution. Reads languageNames from locale JSON files to render human-readable language labels. Generates GitHub blob URLs for docs files based on repoUrl from package.json. Uses docsDir override support to work with per-language subdirectories.

entries.summary: DataSource providing language navigation links and multilingual path resolution for documentation files.
entries.detail: Extends DataSource to generate a language switcher string (🌐 links) for use in docs templates. Implements path resolution logic to compute relative links between docs files across language subdirectories. Loads config.json to determine the default language. Maintains a static LANG_NAMES map for display names.

entries.summary: DataSource providing basic project identity metadata: name, description, and version from package.json.
entries.detail: Extends DataSource and lazily reads package.json from the project root, caching the result. Exposes name(), description(), and version() methods, falling back to directory basename or '0.0.0' when fields are absent. Renders a Markdown table of key project properties via toMarkdownTable().

entries.summary: Minimal DataSource stub for text-related data, currently returning null for all queries.
entries.detail: Extends DataSource with no concrete method implementations, acting as a placeholder. Its presence in the data/ directory allows the data-source-loader to discover and instantiate it, making the 'text' source key available in directive resolution even before functional methods are added.

entries.summary: Defines the AnalysisEntry base class and utility functions for checking emptiness and building category summaries from analysis data.
entries.detail: Exports ANALYSIS_META_KEYS as a Set of top-level metadata keys (analyzedAt, enrichedAt, generatedAt) that are excluded from entry iteration. AnalysisEntry.from() acts as a typed factory. isEmptyEntry() checks whether an entry has any keys beyond the common structural fields. buildSummary() aggregates counts and file lists per category.

entries.summary: Filters analysis entries or full analysis objects by docs.exclude glob patterns.
entries.detail: Converts glob patterns to RegExp via globToRegex() and tests each entry's file path. filterByDocsExclude() operates on a flat array of entries; filterAnalysisByDocsExclude() iterates over all categories in an analysis object and applies the filter per category, preserving ANALYSIS_META_KEYS at the top level.

entries.summary: Resolves which analysis categories map to which chapter files by parsing {{data}} directives in existing docs templates.
entries.detail: extractDataCategories() reads {{data}} directive source names from a template string to identify which categories a chapter uses. buildCategoryToChapterMap() inverts a per-chapter category list into a category→chapter lookup. buildCategoryMapFromDocs() reads all chapter files from the docs directory and builds the full map. mergeChapters() merges preset-defined and config-defined chapter lists respecting ordering.

entries.summary: Centralises resolution of the shared command context object (root, docsDir, config, agent, lang) used by all doc commands.
entries.detail: resolveCommandContext() reads CLI flags and environment, validates config via validateConfig(), resolves the AI agent, and returns a standardised context object. loadFullAnalysis() and loadAnalysisData() load analysis.json from the .sdd-forge output directory. getChapterFiles() lists ordered chapter files from the docs directory, excluding README and layout files. readText() is a safe file reader returning empty string for missing files.

entries.summary: Utility that runs an async worker function over an array with a bounded concurrency limit.
entries.detail: Implements a queue-based concurrency pool using Promise chaining. Accepts an items array, a numeric concurrency cap, and an async worker. Maintains a running count and a results array, resolving only when all items have been processed. Used by enrich.js, forge.js, text.js, and translate.js for parallel AI calls.

entries.summary: Dynamically discovers and loads DataSource module files from a given directory, instantiating each with the project root.
entries.detail: Scans a data/ directory for .js files, dynamically imports each one, and instantiates the default export class with the project root and optional extra arguments. Attaches the source file path as _sourceFilePath for debugging. Returns a keyed sources object. Used by scan.js to load preset-specific DataSources.

entries.summary: Base class for all DataSource implementations, providing shared helpers for description lookup, override merging, and Markdown table rendering.
entries.detail: Defines the _desc() method that checks a loadOverrides() map before returning a default value, allowing per-project customisation of generated text. Provides withSummary() to inject override summaries into entry arrays. toMarkdownTable() renders a two-dimensional array as a Markdown table with a header row and separator. Subclasses extend this to expose domain-specific data methods.

entries.summary: Parses and processes template directive syntax ({{data}}, {{text}}, {%block%}, {%extends%}) in Markdown chapter files.
entries.detail: parseDirectives() scans lines for data, text, and block directives, extracting metadata like preset, source, method, labels, params, and prompt. resolveDataDirectives() iterates directives and calls a resolver function to replace {{data}} blocks in-place. replaceBlockDirective() handles multi-line block replacement. stripBlockDirectives() removes block/extends/endblock comment lines. parseBlocks() extracts named blocks and extend declarations for template inheritance.

entries.summary: Builds AI system and file prompts for the forge documentation-writing command, and converts analysis data to a text summary.
entries.detail: summaryToText() dispatches to enrichedSummaryToText() which formats enriched analysis entries (summary, detail, chapter) into a structured text block for use as AI context. buildForgeSystemPrompt() assembles the system prompt from lang, userPrompt, optional spec content, and the analysis summary. buildForgeFilePrompt() and buildForgePrompt() construct per-file and full-run prompts respectively with round/maxRuns context and review feedback.

entries.summary: Maps file extensions to language-specific parser/handler modules (js, php, py, yaml) for use during source scanning.
entries.detail: Defines EXT_MAP mapping common extensions (.js, .mjs, .cjs, .ts, .php, .py, .yml, .yaml) to their respective language handler modules imported from lang/ subdirectory. getLangHandler() performs a case-insensitive extension lookup and returns null for unknown types. Consumed by the scanner to select the appropriate AST or regex parser per file.

entries.summary: JavaScript/Node.js language handler providing parse, minify, extractImports, extractExports, and extractEssential operations for JS source files. Used by the scan pipeline to extract structural metadata.
entries.detail: Removes block and line comments via regex before minification to produce compact source representations. The `parse` function extracts class name (from filename), parent class, methods, and properties into a structured object. `extractEssential` retains lines containing Node.js built-in usage patterns (`fs`, `path`, `JSON`, `process`, `child_process`) for lightweight context summarization. Import and export extraction uses regex-based scanning of ES module syntax.

entries.summary: PHP language handler providing parse, minify, extractImports, extractExports, and extractEssential operations for PHP source files. Mirrors the JS handler interface for uniform processing across languages.
entries.detail: Handles PHP-specific comment removal including block comments, line comments (`//`), and hash comments (`#`). The `parse` function extracts class name from a class declaration regex or falls back to the filename, along with parent class, methods, properties, and relations. `extractEssential` distills PHP code to key structural lines relevant for analysis context. Implements the same interface as `js.js` to enable polymorphic dispatch via `lang-factory.js`.

entries.summary: Python language handler providing minify and extractEssential operations for Python source files. Implements a minimal subset of the language handler interface.
entries.detail: Minification is implemented as pure hash-comment removal since Python lacks block comment syntax. `extractEssential` filters Python code to retain only structurally significant lines for analysis context. Does not implement `parse`, `extractImports`, or `extractExports`, making it a partial implementation suitable only for minification and essential extraction use cases.

entries.summary: YAML language handler providing a minimal minify function that strips hash-style comments.
entries.detail: Structurally identical to py.js, this handler removes hash comments from YAML content. Used by lang-factory.js to enable uniform minification across supported file types. No parsing or import/export logic is implemented since YAML is treated as configuration data rather than code.

entries.summary: Central minification dispatcher that delegates to the appropriate language-specific handler via `getLangHandler`. Provides a unified `minify` interface for all supported file types.
entries.detail: Resolves the handler for a given file path using `lang-factory.js` and calls either `extractEssential` (when the `essential` option is set) or the handler's `minify` function. Applies common post-processing steps including blank line removal and trailing whitespace stripping regardless of language. Acts as the single entry point for code compression in the scan pipeline, abstracting away language-specific logic.

entries.summary: Utility functions for parsing PHP array syntax: block-comment stripping, key extraction, string extraction, and naming convention helpers.
entries.detail: Provides stripBlockComments, extractArrayBody, extractTopLevelKeys, and extractQuotedStrings to parse PHP associative array literals without a full PHP parser. Also exports camelToSnake and pluralize used for model/table name inference. Re-exported from scanner.js so downstream code has a single import point.

entries.summary: Factory module that creates resolver instances combining preset data sources, overrides, and desc helpers for doc generation.
entries.detail: createResolver loads the preset chain for a given type, instantiates data sources from each preset's data/ directory, and layers project-level overrides from overrides.json. A descFactory provides a closure for looking up override descriptions by section and key. The returned resolver exposes a call(method, analysis, labels) interface used by data.js and text.js commands. Depends on resolveMultiChains from presets.js for preset inheritance.

entries.summary: Parses AI-generated review output into structured results: questions needing input, summary of failures, and per-file pass/fail status.
entries.detail: extractNeedsInput filters question lines from raw agent output. summarizeReview picks the first failure description or returns a fallback string. parseFileResults correlates output lines against a list of all scanned files using basename matching to produce passedFiles and failedFiles arrays. Used by the forge review pipeline to process agent responses.

entries.summary: Defines the Scannable mixin that adds scanning lifecycle methods to a base class.
entries.detail: Implements a class mixin pattern where Scannable(Base) returns an extended class with default implementations returning false or empty Entry objects. Provides a consistent interface for scanner classes across different language presets. No external dependencies; purely structural.

entries.summary: Core file scanning utilities: pattern matching, file traversal, per-language parsing, and file collection used across the scan pipeline.
entries.detail: findFiles walks directory trees respecting exclude lists and subDir constraints. getFileStats reads content, computes a crypto hash, and returns stat metadata. parseFile dispatches to the appropriate lang handler. parsePHPFile and parseJSFile are legacy direct implementations still present alongside the handler-based parseFile. collectFiles uses glob-to-regex conversion for include/exclude matching. camelToSnake and pluralize are re-exported from php-array-parser.js.

entries.summary: Template merging engine that resolves and merges preset-chain templates into final per-chapter markdown content.
entries.detail: buildLayers constructs an ordered list of template directories from the preset inheritance chain. resolveOneFile finds the first matching template file in those layers and returns all source versions. mergeResolved and mergeSourcesAdditive combine parent/child templates by merging directive blocks or appending additive content. resolveTemplates orchestrates full resolution for all chapters, handling multi-chain and fallback language scenarios. translateTemplate delegates to callAgent for language translation.

entries.summary: Detects the test environment and frameworks present in a project from the analysis data.
entries.detail: Defines a TEST_FRAMEWORKS list and inspects analysis entries to identify which frameworks are used. Returns hasTestEnv, frameworks array, and a testCommand string. Used during doc generation to populate testing-related sections in generated documentation.

entries.summary: Builds AI prompts for text-directive generation, including system prompts, per-directive prompts, and batch prompts.
entries.detail: getAnalysisContext extracts relevant analysis fields for a given set of directives. getEnrichedContext reads source files and assembles enriched context with code snippets capped at MAX_CHARS. buildTextSystemPrompt constructs the base system prompt with document style and language. buildPrompt and buildBatchPrompt format individual and batched directive prompts with limit rules. CATEGORY_TO_SECTIONS maps analysis categories to doc chapter sections.

entries.summary: Minimal TOML parser that converts TOML text into a JavaScript object.
entries.detail: parseTOML iterates lines to populate a result object, delegating to parseValue for type inference (string, array, boolean, number). parseArray splits bracket-delimited content into items. Handles only a subset of TOML needed for sdd-forge config files; does not support nested tables or inline tables.

entries.summary: Top-level flow dispatcher that routes flow subcommands to registered command handlers. Resolves project context (root, config, flowState, worktree) before delegating execution.
entries.detail: Parses CLI args and looks up the command group from FLOW_COMMANDS registry. Calls resolveCtx() to build a context object containing root paths, loaded config, flow state, specId, and worktree flag. Uses runEntry() to execute the matched command entry, exiting with EXIT_ERROR on failures. Handles both direct commands and grouped subcommands via a two-level dispatch pattern. Serves as the single entry point for all `sdd-forge flow` invocations.

entries.summary: Implements the merge/PR strategy resolver for the SDD flow, determining whether to skip, create a PR via GitHub CLI, or perform a squash merge based on config and environment.
entries.detail: Reads flow state and config to resolve the merge strategy (skip, pr, or squash). Uses `gh` CLI availability and `commands.gh` config flag together to gate the PR route. Parses the spec file to extract PR title and body content from structured sections. Exports helpers (`parseSpec`, `buildPrTitle`, `buildPrBody`) for use in finalize and other consumers. Throws an explicit error if the PR route is selected but `gh` is not installed.

entries.summary: Generates and saves structured flow reports by aggregating per-phase metrics and formatting them as both JSON data and human-readable text.
entries.detail: The module exports three functions: aggregateMetrics() totals per-phase counters into a flat summary object, generateReport() combines aggregated metrics with redo log data to produce a report payload containing both a data object and formatted text, and saveReport() persists the report as report.json in the same directory as the spec file. formatText() is a private helper that converts the data object into a readable multiline string. The redo log is loaded via loadRedoLog() from a sibling set/redo.js module.

entries.summary: Flow review command that orchestrates AI-assisted code review and test gap analysis. Supports draft/final review modes and an automated test-fix loop with retry logic.
entries.detail: Resolves review targets by reading the spec file and collecting referenced source files. Builds system prompts for draft and final review phases, parses AI proposals, and merges verdict responses into structured results. Implements a test review pipeline: extracts requirements from spec, collects existing test files, generates test design via AI, runs gap analysis, and applies fixes up to MAX_TEST_REVIEW_RETRIES times. Saves review.md and test-review.md artifacts to the spec directory. Exports via runIfDirect pattern for both direct and imported use.

entries.summary: Flow prerequisite checker that validates conditions (impl readiness, finalize readiness, dirty state, gh availability) before step execution.
entries.detail: PREREQS maps target names to required step IDs. checkStepPrereqs verifies that each required step is marked done in flow state. checkDirty runs git status to detect uncommitted changes. checkGh probes gh CLI availability. execute dispatches to the appropriate check based on ctx args and outputs a structured flow envelope.

entries.summary: Context search module for the SDD flow that locates relevant source entries from analysis.json using ngram, AI-assisted keyword, or fallback search modes. Also provides file content reading and phase resolution utilities used during flow execution.
entries.detail: Implements three search strategies: keyword-based text search, bigram-similarity ngram search (threshold 0.15), and AI-assisted search that asks an agent to select relevant keywords from a collected set. The AI path falls back to keyword search on parse errors or agent unavailability. Entries are filtered via filterEntry() and results normalized through toSearchResult(). Phase resolution inspects flow state to determine the current workflow phase. File reading mode reads raw file content from the source root and writes it to stdout.

entries.summary: Retrieves and formats guardrail rules for a given SDD flow phase. Supports both markdown and structured output formats.
entries.detail: Parses CLI arguments to extract phase and format options, validating against VALID_PHASES (draft, spec, impl, lint). Loads merged guardrails via loadMergedGuardrails() and filters them by the specified phase using filterByPhase(). Provides a toMarkdown() helper to render guardrail entries as markdown. Returns results through the flow-envelope ok/fail/output abstraction. Acts as the query endpoint for guardrail inspection within the flow get subcommand.

entries.summary: Flow command that fetches a GitHub issue by number and returns its content as a structured JSON envelope.
entries.detail: Uses execFileSync to invoke gh issue view --json, parses the result, and wraps it in a flow envelope via ok/fail/output. Intended for skill scripts that need to associate issue metadata with a flow.

entries.summary: Prompt retrieval module for the SDD flow that returns predefined prompt strings keyed by language and kind. Exposes an execute() entry point used by the flow dispatcher.
entries.detail: Maintains a PROMPTS_BY_LANG map indexed by locale (e.g. 'ja') and prompt kind, providing a lightweight lookup without file I/O. The validKinds() helper enumerates supported kind keys for validation. The execute() function reads lang and kind from the flow context, resolves the matching prompt string, and returns it via the flow envelope helpers. Unsupported kinds or languages result in a fail envelope response.

entries.summary: Flow command that returns the current QA question count from flow state.
entries.detail: Reads the qa metric from flow state and outputs it as a structured envelope. Used by skills to track how many clarification questions have been asked during a flow session.

entries.summary: Resolves and outputs the full flow context: worktree state, git info, spec content, requirements, and active flow metadata.
entries.detail: Determines the effective root (worktree vs main repo), locates flow.json by specId or active-flow file, reads git state (branch, ahead count, last commit, dirty files) via git-state.js, and extracts spec sections via extractSection. Outputs a comprehensive JSON envelope consumed by skill scripts to build context for AI prompts.

entries.summary: Flow status command that returns the current phase and step list derived from flow state.
entries.detail: Calls derivePhase from flow-state.js to compute the current phase based on completed steps. Outputs phase and steps array via the flow envelope. Consumed by status-display skills.

entries.summary: Defines the FLOW_COMMANDS registry and lifecycle hook factories (`stepPre`, `stepPost`) that wrap each flow command with step status tracking and metric increments.
entries.detail: Provides `stepPre` and `stepPost` higher-order functions that return context handlers updating step status to `in_progress` or a derived terminal state. `deriveActivePhase` delegates to `derivePhase` from `flow-state.js` to determine the current flow phase from loaded state. The `FLOW_COMMANDS` export maps command names to their pre/post hooks and handler modules. Acts as the central wiring point between the flow dispatcher and individual command implementations.

entries.summary: Implements the `finalize` step of the SDD flow, orchestrating cleanup, retro execution, and docs build after implementation is complete.
entries.detail: Exports a `STEP_MAP` for step registration and an async `execute` function that drives the finalize phase. Calls `retroExecute` with `--force` to record retrospective data, then invokes `docs.js` as a subprocess via `runSync`. `executeCleanup` handles worktree removal and returns structured status objects for both success and failure cases. Loads the redo log and generates/saves a report using shared helpers from `commands/report.js`. Handles spec-only mode as an early exit path.

entries.summary: Flow run-handler that validates a spec file against guardrails and optionally uses an AI agent for semantic checks. Exports core check utilities for reuse.
entries.detail: Reads the spec file at the path stored in flow state and runs structural checks via checkSpecText(), which skips exempt sections and flags issues against configurable rules. Calls buildGuardrailPrompt() to assemble an AI prompt from spec text and active guardrails, then parses the structured response with parseGuardrailResponse(). The checkGuardrail() function aggregates results into a passed/failed summary. extractExemptions() parses inline exemption comments from the spec. All core functions are exported for use by other modules.

entries.summary: Flow impl-confirm command that summarizes requirements status and lists changed files to confirm readiness for implementation.
entries.detail: getChangedFiles runs git diff against the base branch to list modified files. summarizeRequirements counts total, done, pending, and in-progress requirements from flow state. execute assembles a confirmation payload including spec existence, requirements summary, and changed file list, outputting via flow envelope.

entries.summary: Flow run-handler that executes lint guardrail checks against changed files. Delegates to the shared lint library and returns results via the flow envelope.
entries.detail: Parses CLI arguments, loads merged guardrails via loadMergedGuardrails(), then calls runLint() with the project root and guardrails. Wraps success and failure outcomes in ok/fail flow envelopes for the orchestrator. Acts as a thin adapter between the flow command interface and the reusable lint library. Keeps command-specific argument handling isolated from core lint logic.

entries.summary: Implements the `flow run prepare-spec` step that initializes a new spec directory with spec.md and qa.md templates, creates a git worktree branch, and writes initial flow state. This is the entry point for starting a new SDD flow.
entries.detail: Generates a sequential spec index by scanning the specs/ directory for existing entries. Creates a feature branch via `git worktree add` or falls back to a regular branch when worktrees are not used. Writes spec.md and qa.md from templates that support local overrides in `.sdd-forge/templates/`. Calls `saveFlowState` with `buildInitialSteps()` and registers the flow via `addActiveFlow`. Enforces a clean working tree and validates the base branch before proceeding.

entries.summary: Implements the `report` step runner for the SDD flow, generating and saving a flow report from the current state and redo log.
entries.detail: Exports an async `execute` function that loads flow state, reads the redo log, and delegates to `generateReport`/`saveReport` from `commands/report.js`. Returns an `ok` envelope with the changed file path (`report.json` under the spec directory) on success, or a `fail` envelope on error. Integrates with the flow envelope system (`ok`, `fail`, `output`) for consistent result reporting. Serves as a thin orchestration layer with minimal logic beyond wiring inputs to the report command.

entries.summary: Flow retro command that runs an AI retrospective comparing the implementation diff against spec requirements.
entries.detail: extractRequirements parses the Requirements section from spec.md. getDiff and getDetailedDiff run git diff against the base branch. buildRetroPrompt formats requirements and diff into an AI prompt. parseRetroResponse extracts structured pass/fail results per requirement. Results are written to specs/<id>/retro.json. Supports --force to overwrite an existing retro.

entries.summary: Thin executor module that delegates `flow review` execution to the `flow/commands/review.js` script via a subprocess and parses its output envelope.
entries.detail: Resolves the review script path using `PKG_DIR`, invokes it with `runSync`, and wraps the result in a flow envelope (`ok`/`fail`/`output`). The `parseTestReviewOutput` function interprets stdout/stderr from the subprocess to determine success or failure. Follows the run-layer pattern used by other flow sub-commands, keeping orchestration logic separate from command implementation.

entries.summary: Flow sync command that triggers a docs build (sdd-forge build) as part of the flow pipeline.
entries.detail: Resolves the docs.js script path via PKG_DIR and invokes it with runSync. Used in the finalize flow to ensure documentation is up-to-date before merging. A thin adapter following the same delegation pattern as run/review.js.

entries.summary: Flow set/auto command that enables or disables autoApprove mode in flow state.
entries.detail: Reads the desired auto value from ctx args and calls mutateFlowState to update the autoApprove field. Outputs result via flow envelope. Used by the sdd-forge.flow-auto-on and sdd-forge.flow-auto-off skills.

entries.summary: Flow set/issue command that associates a GitHub issue number with the current flow.
entries.detail: Calls setIssue from flow-state.js to persist the issue reference in flow.json. Outputs a flow envelope confirming the update. Used after gh issue create to link an issue to an in-progress flow.

entries.summary: Flow set/metric command that increments named counters (question, redo, docsRead, srcRead) in flow state.
entries.detail: VALID_PHASES and VALID_COUNTERS constrain accepted inputs. Uses mutateFlowState to atomically increment the specified counter under the current phase metrics. Used by skills to track AI interaction quality metrics across flow sessions.

entries.summary: Flow set/note command that appends a timestamped note to the flow state notes array.
entries.detail: Delegates to addNote from flow-state.js. Simple single-purpose command used by skills to record observations or decisions during a flow session for later review.

entries.summary: Flow set/redo command that manages a redo log (redolog.json) tracking iteration history for a spec.
entries.detail: loadRedoLog reads from specs/<id>/redolog.json, validating that entries is an array. saveRedoLog writes the log back atomically. execute appends a new redo entry with timestamp and reason, or lists existing entries. Used to track how many times implementation was redone and why.

entries.summary: Flow set/req command that updates the status of a specific requirement by index in flow state.
entries.detail: Delegates to updateRequirement from flow-state.js with the index and new status from ctx args. Outputs a flow envelope. Used by skills to mark individual requirements as done or in-progress during implementation.

entries.summary: Flow set/request command that stores the original feature request text in flow state.
entries.detail: Delegates to setRequest from flow-state.js. Called during flow initialization to persist the raw user request for later reference in spec templates and retro comparisons.

entries.summary: Flow set/step command that explicitly sets the status of a named step in flow state.
entries.detail: Calls updateStepStatus with the step ID and status from ctx args. Used by the registry middleware and by skills to manually override step status when needed.

entries.summary: Flow set/summary command that parses a JSON requirements array and persists it to flow state.
entries.detail: Reads the raw JSON string from ctx args, parses it, and calls setRequirements from flow-state.js. Outputs success or a parse error via flow envelope. Used by the spec-planning skill to initialize the requirements checklist from AI-generated output.

entries.summary: CLI subcommand handler for recording test summary results (unit/integration/acceptance) into flow state.
entries.detail: Parses TYPE_FLAGS from CLI arguments to determine which test category is being reported. Delegates persistence to setTestSummary from flow-state.js. Returns structured envelope responses via ok/fail/output helpers from flow-envelope.js. Follows the standard execute(ctx) contract used by all flow set subcommands.

entries.summary: Help command that prints a formatted list of all available sdd-forge CLI commands with descriptions.
entries.detail: LAYOUT defines the ordered list of command entries. main uses createI18n translate to localize command descriptions keyed by ui:help.commands.<name>. getPackageVersion reads the version from package.json for display. Exported as { main, commands } for use by other modules and the CLI entry point.

entries.summary: Core agent invocation library providing synchronous and asynchronous Claude CLI call wrappers with retry, stdin prompt delivery, and work-dir management.
entries.detail: callAgent uses execFileSync for blocking agent calls. callAgentAsync supports optional retry via callAgentAsyncWithRetry with configurable delay. buildAgentInvocation determines whether to pass prompts via CLI args or stdin based on ARGV_SIZE_THRESHOLD to avoid OS ARG_MAX limits. resolveAgent assembles the full agent config from provider profile and command-specific overrides. ensureAgentWorkDir validates and creates the -C working directory. writeAgentContext and cleanupAgentContext manage temporary rules files injected into the agent's .claude/rules/ directory.

entries.summary: Utility that loads the SDD AGENTS.md template from the base preset's templates directory for a given language.
entries.detail: loadSddTemplate resolves the path to AGENTS.sdd.md under src/presets/base/templates/<lang>/ and returns its content. Falls back to empty string if the file does not exist. Used by the agents command and setup flow to initialize AGENTS.md in new projects.

entries.summary: CLI utility module providing repoRoot, sourceRoot, parseArgs, worktree detection, PKG_DIR, and timestamp formatting.
entries.detail: repoRoot resolves the git top-level directory, preferring SDD_WORK_ROOT env var. sourceRoot prefers SDD_SOURCE_ROOT. parseArgs implements a minimal option parser supporting boolean flags and string values with unknown-option rejection. isInsideWorktree detects worktrees by checking if .git is a file rather than a directory. getMainRepoPath reads the gitdir file to find the main repository root. PKG_DIR is the package's src/ parent, used throughout for script path resolution.

entries.summary: Configuration loading module providing path helpers for the .sdd-forge directory and validated config loading.
entries.detail: sddDir, sddConfigPath, sddOutputDir, and sddDataDir compute standard paths under the project's .sdd-forge/ directory. loadConfig reads config.json and passes it through validateConfig from types.js. loadLang provides a quick lang-only read without full validation. loadPackageField reads arbitrary fields from package.json. DEFAULT_CONCURRENCY and DEFAULT_LANG define fallback values.

entries.summary: Entrypoint guard utilities for distinguishing direct script execution from module imports.
entries.detail: isDirectRun compares the resolved argv[1] path against the module's file URL to detect direct invocation. runIfDirect wraps a main function and calls it only when run directly, exiting with EXIT_ERROR on thrown errors. Used by scan.js, review.js, and other commands that support both direct execution and import.

entries.summary: Defines EXIT_SUCCESS and EXIT_ERROR numeric exit code constants used throughout the codebase.
entries.detail: A minimal constants module exporting 0 (EXIT_SUCCESS) and 1 (EXIT_ERROR). Centralizing exit codes avoids magic numbers and makes intent explicit across CLI commands and flow handlers.

entries.summary: Provides typed envelope constructors (`ok`, `fail`, `warn`) and an `output` function for structured JSON communication between flow command handlers and the CLI layer.
entries.detail: All envelopes share a common shape with `ok` boolean, `type`, `key`, `data`, and `errors` fields. The `output` function serializes the envelope to stdout as pretty-printed JSON and sets `process.exitCode` (not `process.exit`) to allow post-hooks to run after the command completes. `warn` envelopes are `ok: true` but carry a non-zero code and messages to signal partial success. This envelope pattern enables machine-readable output for agent and skill consumers.

entries.summary: Core flow state persistence library managing flow.json files, active flow tracking, worktree resolution, and step/metric mutation helpers.
entries.detail: Maintains two state files: flow.json per spec directory and .active-flow for tracking concurrent flows across worktrees. Provides FLOW_STEPS and PHASE_MAP constants to define the canonical pipeline order and phase transitions. resolveWorktreePaths computes worktree and branch paths from state. scanAllFlows traverses both main repo and worktree specs directories to aggregate all live flows. Mutation helpers (updateStepStatus, incrementMetric, setRequirements, etc.) use mutateFlowState as a single atomic read-modify-write primitive.

entries.summary: Git state utilities providing worktree dirty detection, branch name, ahead count, last commit, and gh CLI availability checks.
entries.detail: All functions use a tryExec helper that returns null on failure rather than throwing. getWorktreeStatus runs git status --porcelain to detect uncommitted changes. getAheadCount runs git rev-list --count to measure commits ahead of the base branch. isGhAvailable probes gh --version with a short timeout. Used by flow commands and skills to gate operations on clean state.

entries.summary: Shared library for loading, hydrating, filtering, and merging guardrail definitions from JSON files across preset chains and project overrides.
entries.detail: Defines hydrate() to parse and attach metadata (phases, lint regex) to raw guardrail entries. loadGuardrailFile() reads a guardrail.json and maps entries through hydrate(). resolveGuardrailContext() determines the active lang and presetKey from project config. loadPresetGuardrails() walks the preset parent chain via resolveChainSafe() and collects guardrails from each level. loadMergedGuardrails() combines preset guardrails with project-level overrides using mergeById(), giving project definitions precedence. filterByPhase() and matchScope() are exported for consumers to narrow guardrail sets at runtime.

entries.summary: Internationalization (i18n) module that loads and merges locale JSON files from package, preset, and project directories. Provides a translation function factory supporting namespaced keys and parameter interpolation.
entries.detail: Loads locale messages from up to three tiers: package-level locale, preset-specific locale, and project-level locale, deep-merging them in priority order. Supports domain-scoped keys in the format `domain:key` (e.g., `ui:button.ok`). Interpolates `{{param}}` placeholders in message strings. Exposes two APIs: `createI18n` (simpler, domain-inferred) and `translate` (full namespaced key support). Preset locale path is derived from the preset key extracted from the config type.

entries.summary: Template include directive processor that resolves `<!-- include("path") -->` markers in template content and expands them with file contents. Enforces path security by forbidding relative traversal sequences.
entries.detail: Resolves include paths through a priority chain: project templates directory, presets directory, package directory, then base directory of the including file. Throws on missing files or circular includes by tracking a visited set of resolved paths. Recursively processes included file content so nested includes are expanded. Path traversal via `../` or `./` prefixes is explicitly forbidden to prevent directory escape.

entries.summary: Lenient JSON repair utility that recovers valid JSON from malformed or AI-generated output. Handles unquoted keys, trailing commas, single quotes, and JSON embedded in Markdown code fences.
entries.detail: Implements a character-level parser that emits corrected JSON as it reads the input stream. Strips Markdown code fences and skips leading non-JSON text before locating the JSON start. Handles edge cases such as unquoted object keys, single-quoted strings, trailing commas in objects and arrays, and bare keyword values. Helper functions detect whitespace, digits, and control characters. The output string is always valid for `JSON.parse`.

entries.summary: Library providing lint guardrail validation, git-based changed-file detection, and content-level lint checks. Used by the flow lint command.
entries.detail: validateLintGuardrails() checks that guardrail entries intended for lint use have valid regex patterns, returning warnings for malformed entries. getChangedFiles() shells out to `git diff` to obtain the list of files changed relative to a base ref. runLintChecks() reads each changed file, applies filterByPhase() and matchScope() to select relevant guardrails, then tests file content against each guardrail's compiled regex, collecting failures. runLint() orchestrates the full pipeline and returns a structured result with ok flag, warnings, guardrail count, file count, and failure list.

entries.summary: Terminal interactive multi-select and single-select UI component that renders a scrollable tree of preset items with keyboard navigation. Used by the setup wizard for preset selection.
entries.detail: Builds a flat list of tree-formatted items from a hierarchical preset structure, indenting child entries with ASCII box-drawing prefixes. Renders a highlighted cursor line, optional ANSI color hints, and contextual keyboard shortcuts to stderr. Supports a single-select mode (enter to confirm) and multi-select mode (space to toggle, enter to confirm). Handles ancestor auto-selection in multi-select, viewport scrolling to keep the cursor visible, and raw TTY mode for immediate keystroke capture. Falls back to returning the first item when stdin is not a TTY.

entries.summary: Preset registry and chain resolution module that discovers available presets from the filesystem and resolves inheritance chains via `parent` references. Provides lookup utilities used throughout the tool.
entries.detail: Discovers presets at startup by reading all subdirectory `preset.json` manifests under `src/presets/`. Resolves a linear ancestor chain for a given leaf preset key, detecting circular references and missing parents with descriptive errors. Supports resolving multiple type strings at once via `resolveMultiChains`, aggregating unique chain entries. Exports `PRESETS` as a module-level constant for O(1) repeated access. `resolveChainSafe` returns a graceful fallback for unknown preset keys instead of throwing.

entries.summary: Thin synchronous process execution wrapper around Node.js `spawnSync`. Provides a uniform return shape for stdout, stderr, and exit status.
entries.detail: Accepts a command string, argument array, and optional spawn options, delegating directly to `spawnSync` from the `child_process` module. Returns a consistent result object so callers do not need to handle the raw spawnSync API. Kept intentionally minimal in line with the no-external-dependencies policy.

entries.summary: Multi-step progress bar and spinner renderer that writes ANSI-formatted output to stderr. Tracks weighted step completion and supports both TTY and non-TTY environments.
entries.detail: Maintains a global progress state object updated across steps. In TTY mode, clears the screen and reserves fixed lines for a header and animated bar, updating them in place using ANSI cursor control sequences. The bar width and spinner frame cycle are constants. Elapsed time is formatted as hours/minutes/seconds. Exposes a `createLogger` factory for prefixed info/warn messages and a `createProgress` factory that returns a step-advance API. Falls back to plain newline output when stderr is not a TTY.

entries.summary: Skill deployment module that reads SKILL.md templates, resolves include directives, and writes the results to both `.agents/skills/` and `.claude/skills/` directories. Skips files that are already up to date.
entries.detail: Discovers skill template directories under `src/templates/skills/` and resolves each `SKILL.md` through the include processor with full path context. Compares resolved content against the existing destination file, writing only when content has changed or the destination is missing. Removes symlinks at destination paths before writing to ensure plain files are deployed. Creates parent directories as needed. Returns a result array indicating which skills were written or skipped.

entries.summary: Defines configuration validation and output resolution utilities for sdd-forge config objects. validateConfig() enforces required fields and throws descriptive errors; resolveOutputConfig() normalizes output settings.
entries.detail: validateConfig() accepts a raw object, checks for required top-level keys, and accumulates errors before throwing a combined message, returning a typed SddConfig on success. resolveOutputConfig() derives a normalized output configuration struct from the validated config. VALID_TONES constrains the docs.style.tone field to a fixed set of accepted values. The module acts as the single source of truth for config schema enforcement across the codebase.

entries.summary: CLI entry point for the `presets` subcommand that prints the full preset inheritance tree. Reads preset manifests and renders them as an indented tree to stdout.
entries.detail: Dispatches on `process.argv[2]` and currently supports a tree-print operation. For each preset, checks whether a `templates/` directory exists alongside `preset.json`. Uses recursive indentation with `isLast` tracking to render box-drawing-style prefix characters. Exits with `EXIT_ERROR` on unrecognized subcommands.

entries.summary: Top-level CLI entry point (`sdd-forge`) that parses the first argument and dynamically imports the appropriate subcommand module. Handles `--help`, namespace dispatchers, and independent commands.
entries.detail: Separates commands into namespace dispatchers (`docs`, `flow`) that receive the remaining arguments and independent commands mapped via a static dictionary to their script paths. Rewrites `process.argv` before importing the target module so each module sees its own argument vector. Prints help and exits cleanly on `--help` or unknown commands. Uses top-level `await` for dynamic `import()` dispatch without a wrapper function.

entries.summary: Interactive setup wizard that initializes a new sdd-forge project, collecting configuration via a terminal prompt and writing `config.json`, gitignore entries, skill files, and an AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md file.
entries.detail: Parses CLI flags for non-interactive operation and loads any existing config as defaults. Prompts the user for project name, source path, language, preset type, docs purpose, and agent provider through a multi-step wizard. Validates and writes `config.json` to `.sdd-forge/`. Creates project directories, updates `.gitignore` and `.gitattributes`, deploys skill files via `deploySkills`, and generates or updates the AGENTS.md (or creates a CLAUDE.md symlink). Handles the `SDD_DIRECTIVE_RE` pattern to update existing AGENTS.md files without overwriting user content outside the directive block.

entries.summary: Upgrade command that re-deploys skills and patches `config.json` when the sdd-forge package is updated. Detects config schema migrations needed for the installed version.
entries.detail: Loads the current project config via `loadConfig` and reads the raw JSON for mutation. Applies any necessary schema migrations to the raw object and writes the updated `config.json` back. Calls `deploySkills` to overwrite outdated skill files with the current package versions. Uses the `translate` function for user-facing messages. Exits with `EXIT_ERROR` on config load failure.
